rationale: The citing paper uses Zhao and Pechmann (2007) as prior empirical evidence that antismoking disapproval messages can reduce adolescents' pro-smoking beliefs and intentions. This is not only background in the introduction; it is also used to justify a methodological choice in Study 1, where the authors omit a disapproval-only condition because that effect is treated as already established. Even though it appears in grouped citations, its function in framing and design support is clear.
relation_description: The 2010 paper treats Zhao and Pechmann (2007) as part of the empirical foundation that disapproval-focused antismoking messaging works among adolescents. That foundation supports both the paper's hypothesis framing and its decision to test mixed-message combinations rather than re-establishing disapproval-only effects.
